message:
fix(services): handle nested hook structures in DCG removal

The previous refactoring simplified the jq program too much. Some hook
configurations have nested .hooks arrays that need to be handled
recursively.

Changes:
- Re-added strip_dcg function for recursive handling
- Handles both top-level command entries and nested structures
- Removes empty hook wrappers after filtering

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -249,13 +249,21 @@ remove_dcg_hook_from_settings() {
 
     local jq_program
     jq_program="$(cat <<'JQ'
+def strip_dcg:
+  if (type == "object" and has("hooks") and (.hooks | type) == "array") then
+    .hooks |= [ .[]? | select(.type != "command" or ((.command // "") | test("dcg") | not)) ] |
+    select((.hooks | length) > 0)
+  else
+    select(.type != "command" or ((.command // "") | test("dcg") | not))
+  end;
+
 .hooks = (.hooks // {}) |
 if (.hooks.PreToolUse | type) != "array" then
   .hooks.PreToolUse = []
 else
   .hooks.PreToolUse = [
     .hooks.PreToolUse[]?
-    | select(.type != "command" or ((.command // "") | test("dcg") | not))
+    | strip_dcg
   ]
 end
 JQ
